summary: Multi-broker (Dhan + Alice Blue + Kotak Neo) backend testing complete. All 26 new tests passing (100%). Verified all 16 review-request scenarios: /api/brokers/status structure, Dhan & Alice Blue credentials CRUD + connect-error messaging, alert routing by broker field, /api/positions/all empty when no auth, ema-sl 'No broker authenticated' guard, existing Kotak endpoints unchanged, and webhook_token persistence across broker changes.
action_items: OPTIONAL: iteration_1's /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py asserts 'Not authenticated with Kotak Neo' in /api/ema-sl/run response. With multi-broker support, server.py now returns 'No broker authenticated. Connect at least one.' That older test (TestKotakCredentials::test_ema_sl_run_not_auth) will fail until updated. Iteration_2 test_multi_broker.py uses the new message and is the source of truth going forward.

OPTIONAL: Dhan error message wraps the SDK dict in str() form ({'error_code': 'DH-901', ...}). Could parse and surface only error_message for cleaner UX, but message currently does include 'Client ID' and 'invalid' so it satisfies the review-request acceptance criteria.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~995 lines - splitting into routers/{auth,kotak,dhan,alice,brokers,webhooks,ema_sl}.py would help maintainability

All three broker clients (kotak/dhan/alice) keep sessions in module-level _sessions dict - lost on backend restart and won't work with multiple uvicorn workers. Acceptable for single-worker preview but should be moved to redis/db-backed cache for production

/api/dhan/credentials and /api/alice/credentials don't validate non-empty input fields server-side beyond strip() - empty strings are encrypted and saved. Pydantic min_length=1 on DhanCredentialsInput/AliceCredentialsInput would prevent storing junk

_route_order() returns broker name in result_note via 'placed/skipped/error' - but the literal broker name in trade_logs comes from cfg_doc['broker'] only via prefix [broker]; the 'notes' returned to the webhook caller include broker tag (good) but the tradelog 'message' field also already prefixes broker so no information is lost
ema-sl run loops positions but does NOT short-circuit if compute_ema10 fails for ALL positions - acceptable but worth a summary status field in response when count==0
Dhan place_order: order_type 'SL' is mapped to 'STOP_LOSS' which in DhanHQ requires both price AND trigger_price. Code passes both correctly. No issue, just noting.
Alice Blue place_order: get_instrument_by_symbol may raise on missing instrument - now wrapped (good). pya3 sometimes returns empty resp - handled.
test_credentials: Re-seeded mongo session: session_token=sess_testdash1234, user_id=user_testdash1234, email=trader.demo@example.com. Cleaned dhan_credentials, alice_credentials, kotak_credentials, user_webhooks before run. Fresh expiry 7d.
seed_data_creation: Test creates TEST_-prefixed alerts (dhan/alice/kotak), TEST_FAKE Dhan + TEST_AB Alice creds. All cleaned up by TestZCleanup at end of each run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration_2 multi-broker tests at /app/backend/tests/test_multi_broker.py - 26 tests, 100% pass. Run: cd /app/backend && pytest tests/test_multi_broker.py -v. KEY VERIFIED: (1) GET /api/brokers/status returns kotak_neo, dhan, alice_blue keys + webhook_token + webhook_url; (2) POST /api/dhan/connect with fake creds -> 400 with 'Client ID or user generated access token is invalid or expired' (DH-901); (3) POST /api/alice/connect with fake creds -> 400 with 'API key not available. Please generate API key'; (4) AlertConfigInput.broker persists & returns; (5) Chartink webhook _route_order() routes to correct broker based on alert config (returns 'skipped - {Broker} not authenticated' when not connected); (6) /api/positions/all returns {positions:[],errors:{}} when no broker auth; (7) /api/ema-sl/run returns 400 'No broker authenticated. Connect at least one.'; (8) user-level webhook_token persists in user_webhooks collection across save/delete of any broker creds; (9) existing Kotak endpoints unchanged. NOTE: iteration_1's backend_test.py needs minor update for ema-sl assertion message - but since iteration_2 has its own test it's not blocking.
